_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of the SIGINT enterprise and its capabilities, limitations, and contributions to cyberspace operations missions.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of MLOps principles and practices, including CI/CD for ML, automated retraining pipelines, model versioning, A/B testing infrastructure, and canary deployment patterns.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-017 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-023 (AI, cosine: 0.438), SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.437).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.0/10 (raw 0.198). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [capabilities, contributions, cyberspace, enterprise, limitations, missions] vs WIDAI-only terms [automated, b, canary, cd, ci, deployment] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.876, R=0.840). P exceeds R by 0.036 — the DCWF element's 15 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 22 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (100.0%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of the SIGINT enterprise and its capabilities, limitations, and contri" and "Knowledge of MLOps principles and practices, including CI/CD for ML, automated r". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.456 > STS 0.198 (gap +0.258). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.8%. Shared tokens: [knowledge]. DCWF-only: [capabilities, contributions, cyberspace, enterprise, limitations, missions]. WIDAI-only: [automated, b, canary, cd, ci, deployment].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 100.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.258 with low STS 0.198. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
